role_title: Product Manager, Link - Local Payment Methods

level_signals: High level of autonomy and responsibility expected
Strategy and roadmap ownership across a major consumer network product

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 8+ years of product management experience, ideally working on a mix of consumer-facing and enterprise products
High taste, design empathy, and sharp judgment — can put yourself in the shoes of consumers and be a steward of crafting great experiences
Rigorous with data — comfortable digging into metrics, writing SQL when needed, and thinking from first principles to deliver the right results
Desire for continuous product improvement; able to optimize for shipping a portfolio of small, medium, and large releases
Ownership mindset — works on whatever it takes to solve problems and delight consumers
Strong written and verbal communication, with a talent for distilling user problems into precise, actionable framing
Experience managing high-stakes external partnerships — aligning on milestones and executing integrations end-to-end
Thrives with a high level of autonomy and responsibility

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience working on consumer financial products
Experience working on local payment methods
Experience building brand new products or working in a startup environment
Experience working with GTM teams to bring a new product to market
Experience working on world-class consumer and merchant products at scale

scope.surface: Link's local payment methods — localized checkout experiences for merchants and consumers globally within Stripe's consumer network (300M+ registered consumers)
scope.business_model: B2B2C — Stripe sells infrastructure to merchants; Link serves end consumers at checkout
primary_metrics_inferred: Consumer conversion rates across local payment methods
Merchant revenue uplift from expanded payment options
Cost reduction for merchants via local methods
core_responsibilities: Define Link's local payment method strategy and roadmap, building localized experiences for merchants and consumers globally
Help establish an ambitious roadmap, from expanding core capabilities to leading nascent initiatives through to maturity
Engage directly with merchants and consumers, analyzing feedback and market trends to inform product development
Champion user insights within Link and represent user needs across the broader Stripe organization
Partner closely with internal and external stakeholders to cultivate a comprehensive, frictionless experience for Link consumers across diverse payment methods
Set the pace for prioritization and execution, partnering seamlessly with Engineering, Design, Data Science, GTM, Marketing, and Operations teams to launch initiatives aligned with company-wide goals

green_flags: Clear product surface and strategic importance — Link described as 'one of Stripe's most strategic consumer bets'
Explicit encouragement to apply if minimum requirements are met, even without preferred qualifications
High-impact scope: 300M+ registered consumers and global merchant footprint
Cross-functional ownership across Engineering, Design, Data Science, GTM, Marketing, and Operations
red_flags: No location or remote/hybrid/onsite policy stated — candidates cannot assess commute or relocation requirements
8+ years minimum is a high bar that may exclude strong mid-senior candidates

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 8+ years requirement and scope of strategy ownership)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description — Stripe is B2B infrastructure, Link is B2B2C)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (not stated in JD; inferred from responsibilities)
location.type set to 'unclear' — no location or work arrangement information provided in JD
compensation.source set to 'unknown' — no compensation information in JD
ambiguities: No location is stated anywhere in the JD. It is unclear whether this role is remote, hybrid, or onsite, and which city or region it is based in.

The minimum requirement bullet about '8+ years...ideally working on a mix of consumer-facing and enterprise products' uses 'ideally' as a qualifier, suggesting the enterprise mix may be preferred rather than strictly required. Treated as hard given its placement under Minimum Requirements.
The JD mentions AI agents and stablecoins as future directions — it is unclear how much of the role currently involves these vs. near-term local payment method execution.
